metadata.title:
LABOR AND CAPITAL LINED UP IN


FIGHT AGAINST THE SHERMAN LAW

President Also Interested and Will


Urge Speedy Changes.

pages.raw_text:
WASHINGTON, d. c., March 81.
The antitrust law must be
amended, and the work must be
done at this session of congress. On
this union labor and union capital
are Of the same mind.


This is the meaning of the con-
ferences at the White House, and
the bringing together of such
strangely assorted persons as
Samuel Gompers and ]. Pierpont
Morgan and Attorney Francis
Lynde Stetson. Morgan wants the
Sherman law amended, and Com
pers wants it amended.


Roosevelt will shortly deliver
special message to congress on the
labor law question as result of
recent conferences with national
labor leaders.


The recent decisions of the
United States supreme court have
brought union labor to see this
question in much the. same light
that Morgan and Hartman view it.
The Sherman law denounces all
combinations in restraint of trade.
This includes alike the operations
of the American Federation of La
Dor and the operations of the Joint
Traffic association.


The law as construed by the
supreme court" said President
Roosevelt. in a recent message to
congress, lis such that the business
of the country cannot be conducted
without breaking it,"


Now, Roosevelt is putting it up
to the men who are interested in
all kinds of combination-whether
it is a labor combination or rail
road combination-that they get to-
gether and amend the law


Pres. Gompers. of the American
Federation of Labor called his lieu
tenants to meet him to consider the
terms of bill amending the Sher-
man law in such way that it will
make perfectly legal the operations
of the American Federation of La
Dor and the other unions of the
country. He explained that If
some such action is not taken, the
results of the Puck stove case and
the Danbury hatter's case will be
disastrous for union labor. lnjunc-
tons and other processes will come
near to putting their organizations
out of business. The conclusions
reached have already be submitted
to Cannon and Fairbanks.


The organized capitalists, on the
other hand, find that they are
breaking the law in their everyday
business operations. One way in
which the law is broken every day
is pointed out by the chairman OF
the interstate commerce commits
sion. who says:


The decision of the supreme
court in the trans-Missouri case and
the joint traffic case has produced
